slides.title: The simplest tool for a longer life: *a daily walk*.
slides.sub: There is a step sweet spot, and it is lower than you think. Here is what the research suggests.

slides.title: Walking is the tool
slides.body: Walking is the *most accessible* longevity tool there is. No gym, no gear, no learning curve, just steps.

slides.title: Aim for the sweet spot
slides.body: Benefits climb up to *about 7,000 to 8,000 steps* a day, then mostly plateau. You don't need 20k.

slides.title: Even a little helps
slides.body: Even around 4,000 steps *beats being sedentary*. The biggest gains come when you go from very few to some.

slides.title: Pick up the pace
slides.body: A *brisker pace* adds a little more. Walk like you're a few minutes late and your heart gets the message.

slides.title: Stack steps into your day
slides.body: Park farther, take calls walking, loop the block after dinner. *Build steps* into the day you already have.

slides.title: Turn daily steps into *real years*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your steps and movement and turns your whole day into one simple, encouraging score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The simplest longevity tool isn't a supplement. It's a daily walk. 🚶

Benefits climb to about 7,000 to 8,000 steps a day, then plateau, so you don't need 20k. Even 4,000 beats sitting, and a brisker pace adds a bit more. 🔖

Vibe Health tracks the steps behind your day and turns them into one score → link in bio.
youtube_title: The Daily Step Count That's Linked to a Longer Life
